title: Grandma's Chicken Soup
description: The cure for everything — a rich, golden broth simmered low and slow with vegetables and egg noodles.
instructions: Place the whole chicken in a large pot and cover with cold water by 2 inches.
Bring to a boil, then skim off any foam.
Add carrots, celery, onion, garlic, and bay leaves. Reduce heat to low.
Simmer uncovered for 1½ hours until the chicken is falling off the bone.
Remove chicken and let cool. Pull the meat off and shred it.
Strain the broth, return it to the pot, and bring back to a simmer.
Add noodles and cook until tender, about 8 minutes.
Return the shredded chicken to the pot. Season generously with salt and pepper.
Serve hot with crusty bread.
notes: Grandma always said the secret is patience — never let it boil hard after the skim.

title: Mom's Lasagna
description: Layers of meaty Bolognese, creamy béchamel, and melty mozzarella. This is Sunday dinner.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F.
Cook lasagna noodles according to package directions. Drain and lay flat.
Brown ground beef and sausage in olive oil. Drain fat. Add marinara and crushed tomatoes. Simmer 20 minutes.
Mix ricotta with eggs, half the Parmesan, salt, and pepper.
In a 9x13 pan: spread a thin layer of meat sauce. Layer noodles, ricotta mixture, meat sauce, mozzarella. Repeat twice.
Top with remaining noodles, meat sauce, mozzarella, and Parmesan.
Cover with foil and bake 45 minutes. Uncover and bake 15 more until bubbly.
Rest 15 minutes before cutting.
notes: Even better the next day. Freezes beautifully — make two and freeze one.

title: Aunt Linda's Banana Bread
description: Dense, moist, and just sweet enough. The riper the bananas, the better.
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F. Butter a 4x8 loaf pan.
Mash bananas in a large bowl until smooth.
Stir in melted butter.
Mix in sugar, egg, and vanilla.
Sprinkle baking soda and salt over the batter and stir in.
Fold in flour until just combined — don't overmix.
Fold in walnuts if using.
Pour into loaf pan and bake 55–65 minutes. A toothpick should come out clean.
Cool in pan 10 minutes before turning out.
notes: Aunt Linda keeps extra bananas in the freezer — thaw overnight when you're ready to bake.

title: Dad's Famous Chili
description: A hearty, smoky chili that's been perfected over 30 years of Super Bowl Sundays.
ingredients: 2 lbs ground beef (or a mix of beef and pork)
2 cans (15 oz each) kidney beans, drained
Sour cream, shredded cheddar, and green onions to serve
instructions: Brown ground beef in a large Dutch oven over high heat. Season with salt and pepper. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, sauté onion and pepper until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic and cook 1 minute more.
Return beef to pot. Stir in tomato paste and cook 2 minutes.
Add crushed tomatoes, beans, chili powder, cumin, paprika, and cayenne.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Simmer uncovered 30–40 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Taste and adjust seasoning.
Serve topped with sour cream, cheddar, and green onions.
notes: The secret ingredient is a square of dark chocolate added in the last 10 minutes. Don't skip it.

title: Nana's Pierogi
description: Potato-and-cheese filled dumplings, boiled then pan-fried in butter with caramelized onions.
instructions: Make the dough: mix flour, sour cream, egg, and salt until a soft dough forms. Knead 5 minutes. Rest 30 minutes wrapped.
Boil potatoes until tender. Drain and mash with cheese, salt, and pepper. Let cool.
Roll dough to ⅛-inch thickness. Cut 3-inch rounds.
Place a spoonful of filling on each round. Fold in half and pinch edges firmly to seal.
Boil pierogi in salted water in batches until they float + 2 minutes more. Drain.
Meanwhile, caramelize onions in 2 tbsp butter over low heat, 20–25 minutes.
In a skillet, melt remaining butter over medium-high heat. Pan-fry boiled pierogi until golden on each side.
Serve topped with caramelized onions and a dollop of sour cream.
notes: Make a big batch and freeze before boiling — drop frozen directly into boiling water, add 2 minutes to the time.

title: Summer Corn Salad
description: Sweet corn, cherry tomatoes, basil, and feta — the taste of August.
ingredients: 4 ears of fresh corn, kernels cut off (or 3 cups frozen, thawed)
instructions: If using fresh corn, you can serve it raw or char briefly in a hot skillet with a drizzle of oil.
Combine corn, tomatoes, red onion, and feta in a large bowl.
Drizzle with olive oil and vinegar. Season with salt and pepper.
Toss gently. Fold in basil just before serving.
notes: Best at room temperature. Add a jalapeño and lime for a Tex-Mex spin.
